BASED ON A TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE SEEN FROM KLOT RADAR DATA, A COMPACT VELOCITY CIRCULATION DETECTED BY FAA TERMINAL DOPPLER RADARS TMDW AND TORD, SPOTTER REPORTS AND PICTURES, AND FIRE DEPARTMENT OFFICIALS, A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 1.9 MILES, AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 60 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE HILTON HOTEL AND WILLOW CREST GOLF CLUB AND ENDED IN CENTRAL PARK IN OAK BROOK. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH WAS CHARACTERIZED BY SNAPPED TREE BRANCHES AND A FEW UPROOTED TREES, AS WELL AS PARTIAL ROOF LOSS TO AN OUTBUILDING. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY THREE MINUTES.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 SW OAK BROOK. REPORT OF A ROOF BLOWN OFF OF A FREE STANDING STRUCTURE AND CARRIED APPROXIMATELY 100 FEET AWAY, IN ADDITION TO A LARGE OAK TREE DOWN AND SEVERAL DOWNED TREE LIMBS NEAR THE HILTON CHICAGO/OAK BROOK HOTEL.

BASED ON REVIEW OF DRONE FOOTAGE PROVIDED BY THE ROCKFORD FIRE DEPARTMENT AND RADAR DATA FROM BOTH THE CHICAGO AND MILWAUKEE NWS RADARS, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT A NARROW SWATH OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 70 TO 80 MPH OCCURRED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 9TH STREET AND BROADWAY SOUTHEAST OF DOWNTOWN ROCKFORD. THESE WINDS CAUSED THE LOSS OF A ROOF TO A BUILDING SOUTH OF BROADWAY WHICH SUBSEQUENTLY RESULTED IN THE COLLAPSE OF THE WEST AND SOUTH-FACING WALLS. ROOFING MATERIAL FROM THIS BUILDING WAS BLOWN IN A NORTH AND NORTHEAST DIRECTION. SEVERAL POWER POLES AND TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG 9TH STREET, WITH ADDITIONAL MINOR ROOF DAMAGE NOTED IN THE VICINITY. THE DAMAGE ORIENTATION WAS CONSISTENT WITH A DIVERGENT AREA OF SOUTHERLY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.THE NWS THANKS THE ROCKFORD FIRE DEPARTMENT FOR THEIR HELP IN THE DAMAGE SURVEY.

THESE WINDS CAUSED THE LOSS OF A ROOF TO A BUILDING SOUTH OF BROADWAY WHICH SUBSEQUENTLY RESULTED IN THE COLLAPSE OF THE WEST AND SOUTH-FACING WALLS. ROOFING MATERIAL FROM THIS BUILDING WAS BLOWN IN A NORTH AND NORTHEAST DIRECTION. SEVERAL POWER POLES AND TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG 9TH STREET, WITH ADDITIONAL MINOR ROOF DAMAGE NOTED IN THE VICINITY. THE DAMAGE ORIENTATION WAS CONSISTENT WITH A DIVERGENT AREA OF SOUTHERLY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.THE NWS THANKS THE ROCKFORD FIRE DEPARTMENT FOR THEIR HELP IN THE DAMAGE SURVEY.
REPORT FROM MPING: QUARTER (1.00 IN.).

BASED ON INFORMATION COLLECTED BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT OFFICIALS, A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 80 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY ONE HALF OF A MILE, AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 60 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BRIAR CLIFF STREET SW AND LIFTED OVER CANDLEWICK LAKE. SEVERAL SOFTWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED AT THE START OF THE TORNADO PATH AND LAID IN A CONVERGENT PATTERN. IN ADDITION, SEVERAL FLOATING DOCKS WERE THROWN ACROSS CANDLEWICK LAKE. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY ONE MINUTE.

THERE IS NO KNOWN DAMAGE CAUSED BY THE TORNADO. ACCORDINGLY, IT WILL BE ASSIGNED A PRELIMINARY RATING OF EF-U. THE TIME IS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY DETERMINED THAT A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN UNINCORPORATED SCHAUMBURG, CROSSED THE ELGIN-O`HARE EXPRESSWAY INTO ROSELLE, AND LIFTED JUST PRIOR TO ENTERING MEDINAH. DAMAGE WAS SPORADIC ALONG THE TORNADO`S PATH AND WAS CONFINED TO TREES.

DAMAGE WAS SPORADIC ALONG THE TORNADO`S PATH AND CONSISTED OF PART OF THE ROOF OF A BUSINESS GETTING PEELED BACK IN TWO DIFFERENT DIRECTIONS WITH THE REST OF THE DAMAGE BEING CONFINED TO TREES.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN FAR SOUTHEASTERN AURORA BEFORE TRACKING SOUTHEASTWARD INTO NAPERVILLE.

INITIAL NWS STORM SURVEY AND DOWNSTREAM TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE ON RADAR CONFIRM A TORNADO IN SOUTH NAPERVILLE WITH AN APPROXIMATE STARTING POINT IN A SHOPPING PLAZA ON THE NORTHEAST CORNER OF ROUTE 59 AND 95TH STREET. INITIAL DAMAGE CONSISTS OF SEVERE SNAPPED TREES AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. ASSESSMENT IS ONGOING WITH ADDITIONAL DETAILS EXPECTED LATER TODAY.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 70 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN FAR SOUTHERN ROMEOVILLE AND TRACKED SOUTHWARD THROUGH CREST HILL AND INTO JOLIET BEFORE LIFTING. FOOTAGE FROM A CONSTRUCTION CAMERA AT LEWIS UNIVERSITY AIRPORT SHOWED THAT THIS TORNADO LIFTED AT LEAST ONCE AND WAS NOT CONTINUOUSLY ON THE GROUND FOR THE ENTIRE LENGTH OF ITS TRACK.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 70 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN THE PRAIRIE BLUFF PRESERVE SOUTH OF LEWIS UNIVERSITY AIRPORT AND TRACKED SOUTHWARD THROUGH CREST HILL AND THE FAR NORTH SIDE OF JOLIET BEFORE LIFTING. DAMAGE WAS ALMOST ENTIRELY CONFINED TO TREES ALONG ITS PATH.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATES THAT A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 80 MPH STARTED NEAR LITTLE ROCK, IL AND MOVED NORTHEAST WHERE IT CAUSED SPORADIC TREE AND POWERLINE DAMAGE TOWARDS BIG ROCK BEFORE LIFTING IMMEDIATELY NORTH OF US 30. THIS TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR LESS THAN 4 MINUTES.
